description: You are a computational chemist maintaining a Python library called `chem_mass` at `/home/ga/PycharmProjects/chem_mass`. The library calculates the molar mass of chemical compounds, but lab technicians have reported incorrect results for compounds like Sucrose and Sodium Chloride. Fix the data error regarding Chlorine's atomic weight in `chem_mass/data.py` and the parsing logic in `chem_mass/calculator.py` to correctly handle multi-digit subscripts (e.g., C12) and parentheses (e.g., Mg(OH)2). Ensure all existing tests pass without modifying the test files.
